content_markdown: An astronaut is orbiting Earth in a spaceship. Why does the astronaut experience weightlessness?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

options.label: A
options.text: The astronaut is outside the gravitational field of Earth.

options.label: B
options.text: The acceleration of the astronaut is the same as the acceleration of the spaceship.

options.label: C
options.text: The spaceship is travelling at a high speed tangentially to the orbit.

options.label: D
options.text: The gravitational field is zero at that point.

vibe_explanation: Weightlessness is experienced when there is no normal contact force between an object and its surroundings. In orbit, both the astronaut and the spaceship are in free-fall towards the Earth with the same acceleration (the local gravitational acceleration $g$). Because they accelerate together, the spaceship does not push against the astronaut, resulting in a sensation of weightlessness.
